mnemonics.title: Mesh Components
mnemonics.content: VEF - Vertices, Edges, Faces - The Three Pillars of Mesh Geometry

mnemonics.title: Manifold Check
mnemonics.content: MANY - Must have All edges shared by Nobody but Two faces, Yes watertight - helps remember manifold properties

mnemonics.title: Data Structures
mnemonics.content: WINGED - Winged-edge Has Its Endpoints Neighboring Each Direction (stores vertices, faces, adjacent edges)

mnemonics.title: Subdivision Types
mnemonics.content: LCCS - Loop for Catmull-Clark works on Curves, Subdivision applies to both

acronyms.expansion: Vertices - Points in 3D space defining mesh shape
acronyms.usage: V = number of vertices in mesh analysis and memory calculation
E is used in Euler's formula and mesh complexity measurement

acronyms.expansion: Faces - Polygonal surfaces bounded by edges
acronyms.usage: F determines rendering complexity and mesh density
Export and import meshes in modeling software
STL files store only triangle mesh geometry without colors or textures
Flexible format for storing mesh data with properties

visualTips.visualization: Draw a cube - count 8 vertices, 12 edges, 6 faces. Apply Euler formula V-E+F=8-12+6=2 ✓
Imagine a bird with wings - each edge has 'wings' pointing to 4 adjacent edges (2 on each side) plus its two endpoint vertices and faces
Think of a two-way street split into two one-way lanes - each half-edge travels in one direction, storing its origin and destination
Triangles = 3 edges forming rigidity (like structural trusses), Quads = 4 edges allowing deformation flow (like character topology)
Start with a rough sketch, divide each line and shape into smaller pieces - the detail increases like pixels in an upscaled image

analogies.analogy: A polygon mesh is like a tent - vertices are poles, edges are the tent poles connecting them, and faces are the fabric sheets stretched between edges.
A closed manifold mesh is like an inflated balloon - the entire surface is continuous with no holes, every point on the surface is part of a complete skin.
A non-manifold mesh is like a cracked eggshell - there are holes, gaps, or places where the surface isn't properly connected.
Subdivision is like zooming into a blurry image - you see more details (more polygons) as you get closer, making curves appear smoother.
GPUs process triangles like factories assembly lines - specialized machinery (hardware) that only knows how to handle triangular pieces efficiently.
